commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make trait derives opt-in via #[derive(...)] on grammar (#10)

* Make trait derives opt-in via #[derive(...)] on grammar declarations

Previously all Types trait associated types required Debug bounds unconditionally.
Moving bounds to where-clauses on impls causes overflow for recursive grammars
due to Rust's inductive trait resolution. Instead, make all derives opt-in:

- Support #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
- Standard traits get manual impls (avoid wrong `A: Trait` bound from derive)
- Serde uses #[derive(serde::...)] with #[serde(bound = "")] to suppress bounds
- No derives by default: Types trait has no bounds on associated types
- Restore bootstrap_regex feature gate removed in PR #9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Restore bootstrap feature gates on meta and regex modules

Gate `pub mod meta` on `not(bootstrap)` and `pub mod regex` on
`not(bootstrap_regex)` so the crate compiles from scratch when the
generated files are deleted, allowing full bootstrap via bootstrap.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add test that verifies bootstrap works from scratch

Deletes both generated files, runs bootstrap.sh, and checks they are
restored identically. Ignored by default since it's slow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix formatting in terminal.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add lease compliance data to chat context and fix cleanup

- Add lease requirements + compliance check results as section 13 in the
  chat context so Policy Insights can answer questions about lease
  compliance, pass/fail status, and specific requirement details
- Fix lease requirement delete to cascade-delete compliance checks
- Add admin cleanup endpoint for stale lease requirements
- Fix AppShell and homepage to treat /?ref=tenant as public route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(data-map): rich per-node context panels with impact paths and actions

Each node in the system graph now opens a detailed drawer showing:
- Impact paths: downstream counts showing how data flows through the system
  (e.g. Projects shows line item counts, milestone counts, runs with metrics)
- Action links: direct navigation to relevant admin pages (Factors, Lineage,
  Calculations, RSP Feed, Test Runs, etc.)
- Node-specific content: Calculator Functions lists all functions with their
  source files, categories, and output metric counts; Compute Runs shows
  status/type breakdowns; Metric Results shows top metric keys by volume

API now returns relationship counts (projectsWithRuns, runsWithMetrics,
pendingChangeRequests, uniqueMetricKeys, metricKeys breakdown) and
calculator function details from LINEAGE_MAP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix school search: add caching, retry logic, and always-visible manual entry fallback

- searchSchools Cloud Function: 24-hour Firestore cache (searchCache collection)
  keyed by sanitized query; cache-hits skip the API entirely. Retry-once on 429.
  Cache writes are best-effort (non-blocking).
- AddSchoolModal redesigned with two always-visible paths:
    • Search field (top) with live dropdown → confirm step → generate
    • "— or —" divider + "Enter full school name" input (bottom) → generate directly
  Manual path bypasses the confirm step; both paths share the same generating/
  progress/success/error states.
- Dedicated 'generating' step renders school name + progress indicators
  regardless of which path triggered it (fixes blank-name edge case)
- Error on generate returns user to the step they came from (search or confirm)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(dx): add ecs-run.sh wrapper for running commands on ECS containers (#249) (#250)

Adds scripts/ecs-run.sh that wraps aws ecs execute-command with sensible
defaults for the dev ingestion worker. Features:
- Finds a running task automatically
- --redeploy flag to trigger ecs-redeploy.sh first
- --service, --cluster, --container overrides
- Clear error messages with troubleshooting steps

commit.message:
Replace hero right panel with real-time clock — always-on concept

Dark panel with a live SVG clock face (real time, red second hand) and
the statement "Running right now." No fake data — the ticking second hand
directly communicates 24/7 infrastructure in a way no copy can.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use stable pod names for consistent hash ring identity (#6)

The hash ring previously used pod IP:port as the node identifier, causing
a full ring reshuffle whenever a server restarted (new IP = new identity).
Now the ring hashes on pod_name (stable across restarts) while keeping the
IP:port for actual gRPC connections.

- Introduce Node struct (pod_name + SocketAddr) in ring
- Membership watcher uses pod name as ring identity, IP for routing
- Convert server Deployment to StatefulSet for stable pod names
- Make server Service headless (required by StatefulSet)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add 10 new features from roadmap

1. Estimated Completion Date - "Finish by [date]" mode in TimeLeftDisplay
2. Reading Heatmap - GitHub-style 52-week calendar on Statistics page
3. Annual Year in Review - shareable year-end reading summary page
4. Typography Presets/Profiles - save/load/delete named reading profiles
5. Storage Size Display - file sizes in library list + total in Settings
6. Quote Sharing Cards - generate styled PNG cards from selected text
7. Reading Badges/Achievements - 15 milestone badges with progress tracking
8. Spaced Repetition Daily Review - SM-2 algorithm for highlight review
9. RSVP Speed Reading Mode - one word at a time, configurable WPM
10. Pomodoro Focus Timer - configurable work/break sessions in reader

feat: integrate Stripe webhooks and enhance payment service

- Added a new webhooks module to handle Stripe webhook events.
- Updated payment service to process checkout sessions and sync after successful payments.
- Introduced new schemas for checkout and callback requests/responses.
- Created a new database table for storing Stripe webhook events.
- Adjusted API routes to include the new webhooks functionality.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reconcile entry events when regatta schedules change

Match saved entries to current RegattaEvents by event_name (stable
identifier) instead of event_number. Auto-updates event_number,
event_time, and event_name in both memory and Google Sheet when the
schedule changes. Warns about orphaned entries whose events no longer
exist. Also adds event_name fallback to entry_matches_event().

fix(money): align remaining 4 files with real Neon money_ledger schema

financial-advisor.ts: 5 queries — replaced entry_type/amount_cents/
status/tag/category/actor_id with event_type/amount_gross/booking JOIN.

t7-escrow.ts: 4 INSERTs — replaced direction/amount/reason_code/
source_ref with outcome_type/amount_gross/notes/external_event_id.

transparent-checkout.ts: 1 INSERT — same phantom column replacement.

fee-utils.ts: 3 INSERTs (dispute hold/resolve/refund) — replaced
entity_type/entity_id/direction/amount/reason_code/source/
idempotency_key with real Neon columns. Uses stripe_refund_id for
refund events.

All amounts now stored as positive (absolute) values — sign semantics
encoded in event_type/outcome_type per Neon schema design.

commit.message:
fix: revert values_callable on DeviceSource — DB uses uppercase names

DeviceSource DB enum has uppercase values (RENPHO_SCALE) matching the
member names, unlike MetricType which has lowercase values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: vertex shader div→mul; font atlas partial GPU upload

render.c — eliminate per-vertex GPU float division:
  The vertex shader converted pixel coords to NDC with:
    vec2 ndc = a_pos / u_screen * 2.0 - 1.0;
  On Mali-G52, fdiv ≈ 20 cycles vs fmul ≈ 2 cycles.
  u_screen now stores the precomputed scale vec2(2/w, 2/h) so the
  shader multiplies instead of divides:
    vec2 ndc = a_pos * u_screen - 1.0;
  Per-frame savings: ~5 100 quads × 4 verts × 2 components = 40 800
  fdiv eliminated.  render_begin_frame updated to pass 2.0f/size.

font.c — upload only baked atlas rows to GPU:
  glTexImage2D previously uploaded the full 2048×2048 = 4 MB texture
  even though 6 font sizes only occupy ~716 rows (35% of the atlas).
  After baking, g_atlas_used_h = g_atlas_cursor_y (~716) and
  glTexImage2D uploads only that many rows — saving ~2.6 MB of GPU
  memory and proportionally faster startup upload.
  UV coordinates in render_bc now divide by g_atlas_used_h instead of
  the compile-time ATLAS_H constant so sampling is correct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move useAssetConfig before early returns to fix hooks order violation

useAssetConfig() was called after conditional returns (loading/error/not-found),
violating React's Rules of Hooks and causing "too many re-renders" crash in production.

[Spark][TEST-ONLY] Fix flaky timestamp test in DeltaCDCSuite (#6213)

#### Which Delta project/connector is this regarding?

- [x] Spark
- [ ] Standalone
- [ ] Flink
- [ ] Kernel
- [ ] Other (fill in here)

## Description

Fix a flaky test "changes - start and end are timestamps" in
`DeltaCDCSuite`.

### Root Cause
The test was using `currentTime + 1` (1 millisecond offset) to create a
timestamp "after" version 0:

```scala
  val tsAfterV0 = dateFormat.format(new Date(currentTime + 1))
```

However, dateFormat uses the format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss Z" which has
second precision only - no milliseconds.

When the formatted timestamp string is parsed back during CDC timestamp
resolution, the milliseconds are lost. This causes flakiness:
- When currentTime does NOT end in 999ms: currentTime + 1 stays in the
same second, formats and parses back to the same second as currentTime +
0. The starting version resolves to v0, test passes with 20 rows.
- When currentTime ends in 999ms: currentTime + 1 crosses a second
boundary (e.g., ...999 → ...000), formats and parses back to a different
second than v0's timestamp. The starting version resolves to v1 instead
of v0, test fails with only 10 rows.

### Fix

Removed the misleading +1ms offset and renamed tsAfterV0 to tsV0 to
reflect that it's intended to be at the same second as v0's timestamp,
not after it.

## How was this patch tested?

Existing test - this PR fixes its flakiness.

## Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing changes?

No

Co-authored-by: Juliusz Sompolski <Juliusz Sompolski>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: 캐시 TTL 설정 외부화 및 X-Process-Time 헤더 추가 (#154)

* feat: 캐시 TTL 설정 외부화 및 X-Process-Time 헤더 추가 (#152, #153)

- Settings에 cache_ttl_seconds, cache_cleanup_interval_seconds 추가
- main.py 하드코딩 상수를 Settings로 이동
- CacheStore.set()에 ttl_seconds 파라미터 추가
- 모든 API 응답에 X-Process-Time 헤더 추가 (초 단위)
- CORS expose_headers에 X-Process-Time, X-Correlation-ID 추가
- 단위 테스트 추가

closes #152
closes #153

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: cache_ttl_seconds 적용 검증 테스트 및 X-Process-Time 형식 테스트 추가

- test_geocode_uses_settings_cache_ttl: geocoder가 settings.cache_ttl_seconds를
  사용하는지 검증 (현재 ttl_days=30 하드코딩으로 실패)
- test_landcover_uses_settings_cache_ttl: landcover가 settings.cache_ttl_seconds를
  사용하는지 검증 (현재 ttl_days=90 하드코딩으로 실패)
- test_process_time_has_six_decimal_places: X-Process-Time 헤더 소수점 6자리 형식 검증

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: cache_ttl_seconds 설정을 실제 호출부에 적용 (#152)

- geocoder.py, landcover.py의 하드코딩된 ttl_days를 settings.cache_ttl_seconds로 교체
- process_time 중복 측정 제거

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix API routes: switch from Edge to Node.js runtime

@vercel/blob requires Node.js APIs not available in Edge Runtime.
Removed `export const config = { runtime: 'edge' }` and switched
to standard Node.js serverless function signature (req, res).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix search crash, Pomodoro counter, and reminder memory leak

- Fix PerformSearch crash: dispatch ObservableCollection updates to UI thread
- Fix SessionsCompleted off-by-one: increment before firing PhaseCompleted event
- Remove duplicate PaletteNames property (ThemePresets already exists)
- Clear _firedReminders HashSet on date change to prevent unbounded growth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Redesign note editor UI: bottom toolbar, new layout, keyboard improvements

- New layout: back button + category pill on row 1, full-width title on row 2
- Bottom toolbar replaces segmented control row: delete (red), share, and
  edit/preview toggle evenly distributed with flexible spaces
- Edit/preview toggle uses UIBarButtonItem (eye = edit mode, pencil = preview)
- Share button sends title + content via UIActivityViewController
- Toolbar slides up with keyboard (animated, 8pt gap) and stays visible
- keyboardDismissMode = .interactive fixes keyboard dismiss on long notes
- Notes open in edit mode with keyboard hidden until user taps text area
- Removed erroneous canPerformAction override (cut crash fix)
- Version bump: 1.2.2 (Build 10) → 1.2.3 (Build 11)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Stripe checkout: remove customer_creation for subscription mode

customer_creation is only valid in payment mode, not subscription mode.
Stripe auto-creates customers for subscriptions.
